channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Global Digital Communications Manager at Madre Brava (Remote) – Drive Climate Impact Through Storytelling
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Madre Brava, a fast-growing international climate organisation, is hiring its first-ever Global Digital Communications Manager—a unique opportunity for a mission-driven professional to shape global conversations on sustainable food systems. If you’re passionate about climate advocacy, digital storytelling, and creating real-world impact, this role could be your next big move. About Madre Brava Madre Brava is […]

The post Global Digital Communications Manager at Madre Brava (Remote) – Drive Climate Impact Through Storytelling appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Madre Brava, a fast-growing international climate organisation, is hiring its first-ever Global Digital Communications Manager—a unique opportunity for a mission-driven professional to shape global conversations on sustainable food systems.

If you’re passionate about climate advocacy, digital storytelling, and creating real-world impact, this role could be your next big move.

About Madre Brava

Madre Brava is on a bold mission: to create a food system that is 100% sustainable, healthy, and affordable for all. The organisation works at the intersection of climate, food, and policy—encouraging companies and governments to shift toward plant-based food systems aligned with the Planetary Health Diet.

With a strong presence across Europe, Madre Brava operates as a remote, agile, and mission-driven team, where innovation, autonomy, and collaboration are deeply valued.

The Role: Global Digital Communications Manager

As the Global Digital Communications Manager, you will play a central role in amplifying Madre Brava’s voice across digital platforms. You’ll help translate research and advocacy into compelling content that informs, inspires, and influences key stakeholders—including policymakers, corporations, and the public.

This is a high-impact, strategic role where creativity meets purpose.

Key Responsibilities

1. Content Creation & Management (60%)

  • Lead the production of engaging digital content (articles, social media, videos, visuals)
  • Write compelling copy for websites and campaigns
  • Design assets using tools like Canva
  • Manage and grow social media channels

2. Strategy & Growth (20%)

  • Monitor and analyze digital engagement metrics
  • Optimize communication strategies based on insights
  • Advise internal teams on social media best practices
  • Strengthen Madre Brava’s digital presence across key markets

3. Innovation & Administration (20%)

  • Stay ahead of digital trends and emerging tools (including AI)
  • Maintain brand consistency and communication guidelines
  • Support internal systems and digital workflows

What Makes You a Strong Candidate?

  • Proven experience in digital communications, especially within climate, sustainability, or food systems
  • Exceptional storytelling and content creation skills (including video)
  • Strong understanding of digital campaigns and audience engagement
  • Experience supporting advocacy or corporate engagement initiatives
  • Ability to work independently in a fast-paced, remote environment
  • Fluency in English (German and/or Spanish is a plus)

Salary & Benefits

Madre Brava offers competitive, location-based salaries:

  • UK: £54,188 – £63,670
  • Germany: €65,025 – €76,404
  • Spain: €57,800 – €67,915
  • Belgium: €64,141 – €75,366
  • Netherlands: €70,555 – €82,902

Additional Benefits:

  • Pension contribution (3%)
  • Monthly WiFi stipend
  • Home office setup allowance
  • Generous paid leave, including organisation-wide breaks in August and December
  • Opportunity to work in a high-impact global climate organisation

Work Location

This is a fully remote role, open to candidates based in:

  • Germany
  • Spain
  • United Kingdom
  • Belgium
  • Netherlands

Application Process

  • Submit your application via the official Madre Brava careers page
  • Shortlisted candidates will go through a multi-stage interview process

Deadline: 20 April 2026 (23:59)

Note: Applications may close earlier due to high volume.

Why You Should Apply

This is more than just a job—it’s a chance to:

  • Shape global narratives around sustainable food
  • Influence corporate and policy change
  • Work with a purpose-driven, international team
  • Lead digital innovation in climate advocacy

If you’re ready to use your digital communication skills to create real impact, Madre Brava wants to hear from you.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more job opportunities, click here. 

The post Global Digital Communications Manager at Madre Brava (Remote) – Drive Climate Impact Through Storytelling appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Afreximbank Internship Programme 2026: Paid Internship Opportunity in Cairo for African and Global Students in Finance, Economics, and Development
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Afreximbank Internship Programme 2026 offers a prestigious, career-defining opportunity for students and young professionals to gain hands-on experience in trade finance, economic development, and international banking within one of Africa’s leading multilateral financial institutions. Hosted by the African Export-Import Bank (Afreximbank), this internship programme is designed to equip participants with practical knowledge, global exposure, […]

The post Afreximbank Internship Programme 2026: Paid Internship Opportunity in Cairo for African and Global Students in Finance, Economics, and Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Afreximbank Internship Programme 2026 offers a prestigious, career-defining opportunity for students and young professionals to gain hands-on experience in trade finance, economic development, and international banking within one of Africa’s leading multilateral financial institutions.

Hosted by the African Export-Import Bank (Afreximbank), this internship programme is designed to equip participants with practical knowledge, global exposure, and professional skills that contribute to Africa’s economic transformation and trade development.

With placements primarily based in Cairo, Egypt, this programme combines professional learning with financial support, mentorship, and exposure to high-level operations within a globally recognized institution.

About Afreximbank

Afreximbank is a multilateral financial institution established to promote and finance intra-African and extra-African trade. It operates through a network of subsidiaries and regional offices, including:

  • Fund for Export Development of Africa (FEDA)
  • Pan African Payments and Settlements System (PAPSS)
  • Afrexinsure (insurance subsidiary)

The Bank is headquartered in Cairo, Egypt, with additional offices across Africa and the Caribbean.

Its core mission is to drive Africa’s trade growth, economic integration, and industrialization through innovative financial solutions and partnerships.

Overview of the Afreximbank Internship Programme

The Afreximbank Internship Programme is designed to provide students with:

  • Practical exposure to trade finance and development banking
  • Insight into African economic challenges and opportunities
  • Experience working in a multicultural and international environment

Key Programme Details

  • Location: Primarily Cairo, Egypt (in-person only)
  • Duration: Minimum 3 months, maximum 6 months
  • Internship Cycles: Twice yearly (January and June)
  • Application Deadline: Open-ended (applications accepted year-round)

Objectives of the Internship Programme

The programme aims to:

  • Enhance understanding of African trade and finance systems
  • Build capacity in structured trade finance
  • Provide real-world experience in development finance
  • Foster innovation through fresh ideas from interns
  • Prepare participants for careers in global finance and development

Eligibility Criteria

The internship is open to a diverse pool of applicants who meet the following requirements:

Academic Requirements

  • Must be enrolled in a full-time undergraduate or postgraduate programme
  • Must provide proof of enrollment in the year of internship

Preferred Fields of Study

Candidates studying in the following areas are strongly encouraged to apply:

  • Economics
  • Business Administration
  • Finance
  • Accounting
  • Social Sciences
  • Statistics
  • Law
  • Engineering
  • Sciences or related disciplines

Nationality Requirements

Applicants must be:

  • Nationals of Afreximbank member countries, OR
  • Students of African descent in the diaspora, OR
  • Non-African students with a demonstrated commitment to Africa’s development

Additional Requirements

  • Age: Between 20 and 32 years
  • Language: Fluency in English or French
    • Working knowledge of the other language is required
    • Knowledge of Arabic or Portuguese is an advantage
  • Must not be a child of Afreximbank staff

Internship Structure and Experience

Interns are fully integrated into the Bank’s operations and receive structured learning experiences.

Key Features

  1. Induction Programme
    • Introduction to Afreximbank’s operations and policies
  2. Department Placement
    • Assignment to a primary department
    • Mentorship by experienced professionals
  3. Rotational Exposure
    • Opportunities to work across multiple departments
    • Exposure to different aspects of the Bank’s value chain
  4. Final Report
    • Interns must submit a report outlining their learning and contributions

Application Process

Applicants must complete an Online Application and submit the following documents:

Required Documents

  • Letter of application
  • Updated CV
  • Valid passport copy
  • Certified academic certificates
  • Recommendation letter from academic institution
  • Statement of Intent (maximum 1 page)
  • Application Deadline: 30th April, 2026

Internship Acceptance Requirements

Once selected, candidates must:

  • Accept the offer within two weeks
  • Provide proof of:
    • Medical insurance
    • Personal accident insurance

Failure to meet these requirements will result in withdrawal of the offer.

Financial Benefits and Support

Although interns are responsible for their general upkeep, Afreximbank provides substantial support.

Financial Support Includes

  • Monthly stipend: USD 1,000 (tax-free)
  • Housing allowance: USD 500 (for international interns)
  • Travel costs: Covered (return airfare)
  • One-week hotel accommodation (bed and breakfast) upon arrival
  • Airport transfers
  • Visa support (if required)

Responsibilities of Interns

Interns are expected to:

  • Demonstrate professionalism and commitment
  • Adhere to the Bank’s policies and regulations
  • Contribute meaningfully to assigned tasks
  • Complete all programme requirements

Important Conditions

  • Internship is not virtual; relocation is required
  • Interns are not considered employees
  • Internship does not guarantee employment
  • Internship can only be undertaken once
  • Duration must be between 3 and 6 months

Why You Should Apply

The Afreximbank Internship Programme offers a unique opportunity to:

  • Gain experience in international finance and development
  • Work with a leading African financial institution
  • Build global networks
  • Develop practical and technical skills
  • Contribute to Africa’s economic transformation

Learn more and apply HERE.

Discover more global Internship opportunities on OFY.

The post Afreximbank Internship Programme 2026: Paid Internship Opportunity in Cairo for African and Global Students in Finance, Economics, and Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Kapor Foundation Research Fellowship 2026: $35,000 Funding for Journalists & Tech Policy Researchers Driving Equity in Tech
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The Kapor Foundation has officially opened applications for its highly impactful Research Fellowship, offering substantial funding to journalists and researchers committed to uncovering inequities and shaping the future of responsible technology. This opportunity is designed for professionals whose work drives meaningful change in areas like AI ethics, tech policy, and inclusive innovation. About the Kapor […]

The post Kapor Foundation Research Fellowship 2026: $35,000 Funding for Journalists & Tech Policy Researchers Driving Equity in Tech appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Kapor Foundation has officially opened applications for its highly impactful Research Fellowship, offering substantial funding to journalists and researchers committed to uncovering inequities and shaping the future of responsible technology. This opportunity is designed for professionals whose work drives meaningful change in areas like AI ethics, tech policy, and inclusive innovation.

About the Kapor Foundation Research Fellowship

The Kapor Foundation Research Fellowship supports investigative journalists and tech policy researchers working on projects that address systemic inequities in the technology ecosystem. Through this initiative, the foundation aims to deepen understanding, inform policy decisions, and promote equitable solutions in tech.

Selected fellows will receive $35,000 in funding to support their research or investigative reporting projects.

Applications are accepted on a rolling basis, with the first award notifications expected by June 30, 2026.

Focus Areas

Applicants must align their projects with one or more of the following priority areas:

1. CS/AI Education

  • Expanding access to computer science and AI education
  • Addressing disparities in tech learning pathways
  • Evaluating policies that support responsible AI education

2. Innovation

  • Advancing responsible AI and tech innovation
  • Examining risks and benefits of AI investments
  • Supporting inclusive entrepreneurship ecosystems

3. Governance

  • Strengthening tech accountability and regulation
  • Addressing algorithmic bias and data privacy concerns
  • Protecting marginalized communities from tech-related harms

Funding & Benefits

  • Award Amount: $35,000 per fellow
  • Total Awards: Up to 15 fellows selected
  • Project Scope: National or region-specific (Oakland, Atlanta, Detroit)
  • Output: Published investigative reports or policy research

This fellowship empowers recipients to produce in-depth, high-impact work that contributes to policy development and systemic change in the tech industry.

Eligibility Criteria

For Investigative Reporting Fellows

  • Journalists (staff or freelance) based in the United States
  • Minimum of 5 years of investigative reporting experience
  • Must secure a publishing commitment from a media outlet

For Tech Policy Research Fellows

  • Researchers affiliated with U.S.-based research or policy institutes (501c3 organizations)
  • Experience in CS/AI education or tech policy research

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  • Personal bio (max 250 words)
  • CV or resume
  • Two published work samples
  • Project proposal (2–5 pages)
  • Project timeline and dissemination plan
  • Research summary/abstract
  • Supporting documentation (including editor commitment for journalists)

All documents must follow specified formatting guidelines and be submitted via the official online application portal.

Fellowship Expectations

Fellows are expected to:

  • Deliver high-quality, publishable research or investigative reports
  • Submit progress updates and final outputs
  • Acknowledge Kapor Foundation support in publications
  • Share dissemination strategies to maximize impact

Why You Should Apply

This fellowship is more than funding—it’s a platform to influence the future of technology. If your work challenges systemic inequities, promotes ethical AI, or informs impactful tech policy, this is your chance to make a difference at scale.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should prepare all required documents and submit their applications through the official Kapor Foundation portal.

Deadline: Rolling basis (First awards announced by June 30, 2026)

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

The post Kapor Foundation Research Fellowship 2026: $35,000 Funding for Journalists & Tech Policy Researchers Driving Equity in Tech appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: D-Prize Global Competition 2026: Win Up to $20,000 to Launch a Social Impact Organization Fighting Poverty Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The D-Prize Global Competition 2026 is now open, offering aspiring entrepreneurs the opportunity to launch new organizations that deliver proven poverty solutions to underserved communities. With funding of up to $20,000, D-Prize supports early-stage founders who are ready to pilot and scale impactful interventions that address some of the world’s most pressing challenges. Unlike traditional […]

The post D-Prize Global Competition 2026: Win Up to $20,000 to Launch a Social Impact Organization Fighting Poverty Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The D-Prize Global Competition 2026 is now open, offering aspiring entrepreneurs the opportunity to launch new organizations that deliver proven poverty solutions to underserved communities. With funding of up to $20,000, D-Prize supports early-stage founders who are ready to pilot and scale impactful interventions that address some of the world’s most pressing challenges.

Unlike traditional grants or fellowships, D-Prize focuses specifically on distribution—ensuring that existing, evidence-based solutions reach the people who need them most. This makes it one of the most unique and impactful global competitions for social entrepreneurs.

About D-Prize

D-Prize is a global organization dedicated to fighting extreme poverty by funding new organizations that distribute proven solutions. Its core philosophy is simple yet powerful: many solutions to poverty already exist, but they are not reaching enough people, especially in last-mile communities.

Through its annual competition, D-Prize identifies and supports entrepreneurs who can bridge this gap by scaling access to these solutions.

What the D-Prize Competition Offers

The D-Prize Global Competition provides selected entrepreneurs with funding and support to launch and grow their organizations.

Key Benefits

  • Up to $20,000 in seed funding
  • Opportunity to launch a new social impact organization
  • Access to a global network of social entrepreneurs
  • Support to pilot and scale impactful interventions
  • Exposure to funders and global development stakeholders

Focus Areas and Challenges

D-Prize invites applicants to choose from a range of evidence-based interventions across multiple sectors. Each challenge focuses on scaling solutions that have already been proven effective.

Key Sectors Include

  1. Agriculture
  2. Education
  3. Energy
  4. Health
  5. Livelihoods
  6. Public Services
  7. Water

Example Challenges

Some of the featured challenges for 2026 include:

  • Distribution of contraceptives (DMPA-SC)
  • Access to reading glasses for underserved populations
  • Expanding access to childcare services
  • Phone-based tutoring for education improvement
  • Teaching foundational literacy and numeracy
  • Road safety campaigns
  • Government transparency initiatives
  • Poverty graduation programs
  • Post-harvest agricultural support

Applicants can also propose their own distribution model for proven interventions not listed in the official challenges.

Application Process

The D-Prize application process is structured in multiple stages to identify the most promising entrepreneurs.

Step 1: Concept Note Submission

Applicants submit:

  • A two-page concept note
  • Resume(s) of team members

D-Prize receives over 3,000 applications each year, making this a highly competitive stage.

Step 2: Shortlisted Application

Top 5% of applicants are invited to:

  • Respond to additional written questions
  • Provide more detailed plans

Applicants are given approximately two weeks to complete this stage.

Step 3: Final Interviews

Top candidates proceed to:

  • Phone interviews
  • Email-based evaluations

Step 4: Award and Launch

Selected finalists (top 1%) receive:

  • Up to $20,000 in funding
  • Support to pilot their organization
  • Guidance on scaling operations

Download an Application Packet

Eligibility Criteria

D-Prize is specifically designed for early-stage entrepreneurs.

Who Can Apply

Applicants must:

  • Be launching a new organization
  • Be early-stage founders (often first-time entrepreneurs)
  • Have not raised significant external funding

Key Requirements

  • Organizations should not have:
    • More than 18 months of full-time operations
    • Raised more than $30,000 in funding
  • Existing organizations may apply only if:
    • Their focus is on distributing proven interventions

Target Applicants

D-Prize strongly encourages:

  • Applicants from low- and middle-income countries
  • Individuals committed to social impact
  • Entrepreneurs with scalable ideas

Why D-Prize is Unique

D-Prize stands out because it focuses on scaling what already works rather than reinventing solutions.

Key Advantages

  • Focus on proven, evidence-based interventions
  • Emphasis on real-world impact and scalability
  • Support for first-time entrepreneurs
  • Opportunity to build organizations from scratch
  • Strong global recognition and credibility

Timeline and Deadlines

The D-Prize Global Competition 2026 follows a structured timeline.

Key Dates

  • Competition Launch: April 7, 2026
  • Early Deadline: May 17, 2026
  • Regular Deadline: June 7, 2026
  • Extension Deadline: June 28, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early to increase their chances of success.

Register for an extension.

For more, please see Frequently Asked Questions.

Tips for a Strong Application

To increase your chances of success:

  • Focus on a clear and scalable distribution model
  • Choose a proven intervention with measurable impact
  • Demonstrate understanding of the target community
  • Highlight your ability to execute the idea
  • Present a realistic pilot plan

Get more information about the D-Prize Global Competition 2026 HERE.

Discover more other global Competition and Grant opportunities on OFY.

The post D-Prize Global Competition 2026: Win Up to $20,000 to Launch a Social Impact Organization Fighting Poverty Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: National Geographic Society × Climate Pledge Funding Program 2026: Up to $100,000 for Climate Storytelling Projects
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The National Geographic Society, in partnership with The Climate Pledge, has opened applications for a powerful funding opportunity supporting storytelling on climate resilience, energy transition, and sustainability solutions. This program is designed to amplify impactful stories that can influence global conversations, shape policies, and drive action on climate issues. About the Program The funding initiative […]

The post National Geographic Society × Climate Pledge Funding Program 2026: Up to $100,000 for Climate Storytelling Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The National Geographic Society, in partnership with The Climate Pledge, has opened applications for a powerful funding opportunity supporting storytelling on climate resilience, energy transition, and sustainability solutions.

This program is designed to amplify impactful stories that can influence global conversations, shape policies, and drive action on climate issues.

About the Program

The funding initiative supports storytellers, researchers, and changemakers who are using media and storytelling to highlight solutions to climate challenges.

Projects can span various formats, including journalism, photography, film, and multimedia storytelling—focusing on real-world solutions and innovation.

What You’ll Receive

💰 Up to $100,000 in funding
📅 2-year project support
🎓 Access to the National Geographic Explorers community, tools, and training
🌍 Global visibility to influence policymakers and decision-makers

Who Should Apply

This opportunity is ideal for:

Storytellers and journalists
Filmmakers and photographers
Researchers and environmental advocates
Creative professionals working on climate-related topics

Applicants should demonstrate:

A strong storytelling concept focused on climate solutions
Ability to execute and deliver impactful projects
Interest in sustainability, energy transition, or climate resilience

Why This Opportunity Matters

Storytelling plays a critical role in shaping how people understand and respond to climate change. This program empowers individuals to highlight solutions, amplify voices, and inspire action on a global scale.

How to Apply

🔗 Learn more and apply.

For more Grant opportunities, Visit OFY here

The post National Geographic Society × Climate Pledge Funding Program 2026: Up to $100,000 for Climate Storytelling Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Macy’s Social Impact Summer Internship 2026: Work on Cause Marketing & Corporate Giving
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The Macy’s Social Impact team is hiring a Summer Intern to support initiatives across cause marketing, corporate giving, and employee volunteering. This is a great opportunity for students who are passionate about making a difference and want hands-on experience in corporate social responsibility (CSR). About the Internship The selected intern will work closely with the […]

The post Macy’s Social Impact Summer Internship 2026: Work on Cause Marketing & Corporate Giving appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Macy’s Social Impact team is hiring a Summer Intern to support initiatives across cause marketing, corporate giving, and employee volunteering.

This is a great opportunity for students who are passionate about making a difference and want hands-on experience in corporate social responsibility (CSR).

About the Internship

The selected intern will work closely with the Social Impact team, gaining exposure to how a major global brand drives meaningful social change through its programs.

The role focuses on supporting initiatives that connect business goals with community impact.

What You’ll Work On

Cause marketing campaigns
Corporate giving initiatives
Employee/colleague volunteering programs
Project coordination and research across social impact activities

Who Should Apply

This opportunity is ideal for college students who:

Have a strong passion for giving back
Have experience in volunteering, fundraising, or community service
Are interested in social impact, nonprofit work, or CSR

There is no specific major required—your commitment to impact matters most.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This internship offers real-world experience in how companies integrate business strategy with social good, making it perfect for students interested in careers in CSR, nonprofits, or impact-driven roles.

How to Apply

🔗 Apply here.

For more internship opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post Macy’s Social Impact Summer Internship 2026: Work on Cause Marketing & Corporate Giving appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Blue Food & Aquaculture Innovation Program 2026: Scale Your Startup in Sustainable Food Systems
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for a high-impact startup accelerator program focused on aquaculture and blue food innovation—supporting entrepreneurs building solutions for a more sustainable and resilient food future. Whether you’re refining your product, seeking investment, or preparing to scale, this program provides the tools, mentorship, and network needed to grow your venture. About the Program […]

The post Blue Food & Aquaculture Innovation Program 2026: Scale Your Startup in Sustainable Food Systems appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for a high-impact startup accelerator program focused on aquaculture and blue food innovation—supporting entrepreneurs building solutions for a more sustainable and resilient food future.

Whether you’re refining your product, seeking investment, or preparing to scale, this program provides the tools, mentorship, and network needed to grow your venture.

About the Program

This initiative is designed to empower startups working within the blue economy, particularly in aquaculture and sustainable food systems. It supports founders tackling challenges such as food security, climate resilience, and sustainable resource management.

Participants will join a transformative journey that combines hands-on support, expert mentorship, and access to industry ecosystems.

What You’ll Gain

Hands-on mentorship from industry experts
Access to investors and strategic partners
Guidance on product development and scaling
Exposure to global opportunities in sustainable food systems
Support in refining business models and go-to-market strategies

Who Should Apply

This program is ideal for:

Early-stage startups developing innovative aquaculture or blue food solutions
Growth-stage startups seeking to scale operations
Entrepreneurs working on sustainable food systems, climate, or marine innovation

Applicants should demonstrate:

A clear product or solution in aquaculture or blue food
Strong interest in sustainability and innovation
Commitment to scaling impact-driven solutions

Why This Opportunity Matters

As global demand for sustainable food increases, aquaculture and blue food systems are becoming critical to feeding the future. This program supports innovators who are building scalable, climate-smart solutions that can transform how food is produced and distributed.

Application Deadline

📅 April 30, 2026

How to Apply

🔗 Apply here.

For more Accelerator Opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post Blue Food & Aquaculture Innovation Program 2026: Scale Your Startup in Sustainable Food Systems appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Harmony in Diversity Fund 2026: Empowering Community-Led Initiatives for Social Cohesion Across Asia
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Harmony in Diversity Fund 2026 is a transformative funding opportunity designed to strengthen social cohesion and promote inclusive societies across Asia. Launched through a strategic partnership between AVPN, Temasek Foundation, Silent Foundation, and the Nagulendran Philanthropy Alliance, this initiative is focused on bridging cultural divides, fostering collaboration, and supporting grassroots organisations that are actively […]

The post Harmony in Diversity Fund 2026: Empowering Community-Led Initiatives for Social Cohesion Across Asia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Harmony in Diversity Fund 2026 is a transformative funding opportunity designed to strengthen social cohesion and promote inclusive societies across Asia. Launched through a strategic partnership between AVPN, Temasek Foundation, Silent Foundation, and the Nagulendran Philanthropy Alliance, this initiative is focused on bridging cultural divides, fostering collaboration, and supporting grassroots organisations that are actively building unity in diverse communities.

In an increasingly interconnected yet fragmented world, social cohesion is no longer optional; it is essential. The Harmony in Diversity Fund recognizes that meaningful change begins at the community level. By empowering organisations that work directly with people from different cultural, ethnic, and social backgrounds, the Fund seeks to cultivate trust, mutual understanding, and long-term collaboration across Asia.

About the Harmony in Diversity Fund

The Fund is built on the belief that diversity should be celebrated and leveraged as a strength rather than a source of division. It aims to bring together stakeholders from across sectors; including philanthropic organisations, private institutions, public bodies, and grassroots communities; to collectively mobilize resources and scale impactful initiatives.

Through this collaborative approach, the Fund supports innovative and scalable projects that promote harmony and inclusivity. It also serves as a platform for knowledge-sharing and partnership-building, enabling organisations to learn from one another and amplify their impact.

Key Objectives

The Harmony in Diversity Fund is designed to:

  • Foster stronger relationships among diverse communities
  • Promote intercultural understanding and respect
  • Build capacity within communities to sustain harmony initiatives
  • Encourage innovation in addressing social cohesion challenges
  • Strengthen cross-sector partnerships for long-term impact

Eligibility Criteria

To be considered for funding, organisations must meet the following requirements:

  • Be a legally registered non-profit organisation or social enterprise
  • Operate within Southeast Asia
  • Demonstrate active involvement in harmony or social cohesion initiatives
  • Provide at least two recent years of audited financial reports

However, the following entities are not eligible:

  • Religious organisations
  • Politically affiliated organisations
  • Organisations listed under US or EU sanctions or blacklisted by international agencies

Impact Areas

Applicants are encouraged to propose initiatives that fall within one or more of the following focus areas:

  • Promoting intercultural understanding
  • Strengthening collaboration among diverse groups
  • Building community capacity for harmony initiatives
  • Developing creative and innovative solutions to advance social cohesion

Projects can be at an early stage of development or ready to scale, as long as they demonstrate clear potential for impact and sustainability.

Funding and Support

While specific funding amounts are not explicitly stated, selected organisations will receive financial support along with opportunities for partnership, visibility, and ecosystem engagement. The Fund emphasizes not just financial aid, but also the strategic support needed to grow and sustain impactful initiatives over time.

Application Timeline

  • 16 March 2026 – Call for Applications Opens
  • 25 March 2026 – Virtual Information Session 1
  • 8 April 2026 – Virtual Information Session 2
  • May – July 2026 – Review Period
  • August 2026 – Announcement of Selected Grantees

Application Process

Applications are submitted through the Good Grants platform. Each submission will be evaluated by a panel of experts based on:

  • Relevance to the Fund’s mission
  • Innovation and creativity
  • Feasibility and scalability
  • Potential for long-term impact
  • Strength of partnerships and community engagement

Applicants may also apply as a coalition, with one lead partner representing the group. This collaborative approach is encouraged and aligns with the Fund’s emphasis on partnership and shared impact.

Why This Fund Matters

Social cohesion does not happen by chance—it is intentionally built through trust, dialogue, and sustained effort. Across Asia, many organisations are already doing incredible work to bridge divides, often with limited resources. The Harmony in Diversity Fund seeks to amplify these efforts by providing the support needed to expand their reach and deepen their impact.By investing in community-led initiatives, the Fund contributes to a future where diversity is not just acknowledged but embraced as a foundation for stronger, more resilient societies.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here. 

The post Harmony in Diversity Fund 2026: Empowering Community-Led Initiatives for Social Cohesion Across Asia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Host a Global Stage: Apply to Host the Seventh International Conference on Learning Cities (ICLC 7) in 2027
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The UNESCO Global Network of Learning Cities (GNLC) has officially opened its call for applications for cities interested in hosting the Seventh International Conference on Learning Cities (ICLC 7). Scheduled for June 2027, this highly anticipated global gathering will bring together leaders, policymakers, researchers, and practitioners to shape the future of urban learning in an […]

The post Host a Global Stage: Apply to Host the Seventh International Conference on Learning Cities (ICLC 7) in 2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UNESCO Global Network of Learning Cities (GNLC) has officially opened its call for applications for cities interested in hosting the Seventh International Conference on Learning Cities (ICLC 7). Scheduled for June 2027, this highly anticipated global gathering will bring together leaders, policymakers, researchers, and practitioners to shape the future of urban learning in an era increasingly influenced by artificial intelligence.

Under the forward-looking working theme “Learning Cities in the Age of AI: Towards Inclusive Digital Urban Futures,” ICLC 7 aims to explore how cities can harness the transformative power of AI to promote inclusive growth, lifelong learning, and sustainable urban development.

About the International Conference on Learning Cities (ICLC 7)

The International Conference on Learning Cities is one of the flagship events of the UNESCO Global Network of Learning Cities. It serves as a global platform for exchanging knowledge, sharing best practices, and strengthening collaboration among cities committed to lifelong learning.ICLC 7 will focus on how artificial intelligence is reshaping urban life ;from education systems to workforce development ;and how cities can strategically adapt to ensure that no one is left behind in the digital transition.Participants will engage in discussions that highlight innovative approaches to:

  • Building sustainable and inclusive learning ecosystems
  • Integrating digital technologies into education and governance
  • Advancing the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) at the local level
  • Strengthening lifelong learning frameworks within urban environments

A Strategic Moment in the GNLC 2027/2028 Cycle

ICLC 7 is more than just a conference; it is a key milestone in the upcoming GNLC thematic cycle for 2027/2028. This cycle places strong emphasis on the intersection of AI, urban development, and lifelong learning.Through participation and hosting, cities will gain access to:

  • Practical tools for leveraging digital innovation
  • Strategies for strengthening lifelong learning policies
  • Insights into building resilient, inclusive, and smart urban communities

This makes the conference a pivotal opportunity for cities to align their local development agendas with global priorities.

Why Your City Should Host ICLC 7

Hosting ICLC 7 is a prestigious opportunity that offers both immediate and long-term benefits. Selected host cities will not only gain international recognition but also position themselves as leaders in the global learning movement.

Key advantages include:

  1. Global Visibility and Recognition
    Hosting elevates your city’s profile on the international stage as a champion of lifelong learning and innovation.
  2. Strengthened Partnerships
    Cities will have the opportunity to build and deepen relationships with global stakeholders, including governments, institutions, and development partners.
  3. Local Development Impact
    The event can catalyze local innovation, inspire policy reforms, and enhance educational and digital infrastructure.
  4. Investment and Collaboration Opportunities
    Hosting attracts international attention, opening doors to funding, partnerships, and collaborative projects.
  5. Community Engagement
    It provides a platform to involve local communities in a shared vision of learning, inclusion, and sustainable growth.

What the Conference Will Deliver

ICLC 7 will provide a dynamic and engaging platform to:

  • Showcase successful learning city models and practices
  • Review progress made across GNLC member cities
  • Promote integrated approaches to lifelong learning
  • Encourage knowledge-sharing across regions and sectors

The conference is expected to gather a diverse audience, ensuring a rich exchange of ideas and experiences that will shape the future of learning cities globally.

How to Apply

Cities that are members of the GNLC are invited to submit their applications to learningcities@unesco.org to host ICLC 7.

Application Requirements:

  • A completed application form (available in Word or PDF format)
  • Official endorsement from the city’s mayor

Submission Email:

learningcities@unesco.org

Application Deadline:

15 May 2026

Late or incomplete applications may not be considered, so early preparation is strongly advised.

As cities around the world navigate rapid technological change, ICLC 7 presents a unique opportunity to lead conversations that will define the future of urban living. Hosting this conference is not just about organizing an event; it is about shaping a global vision for inclusive, intelligent, and sustainable cities.Cities ready to take bold steps toward innovation and lifelong learning are strongly encouraged to apply and be part of this transformative journey.

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here.

The post Host a Global Stage: Apply to Host the Seventh International Conference on Learning Cities (ICLC 7) in 2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded Postdoctoral Fellowship in Political Science in Germany 2026 (Max Planck Institute) – Salary, Requirements, and How to Apply
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Max Planck Institute for Political and Social Science in Göttingen, Germany, is inviting applications for a Postdoctoral Fellow (m/f/d) in Political Science within its newly established Department of Political Institutions and Conflict. This is a full-time, three-year research position designed for highly qualified scholars who are interested in interdisciplinary, data-driven research on political institutions, […]

The post Fully Funded Postdoctoral Fellowship in Political Science in Germany 2026 (Max Planck Institute) – Salary, Requirements, and How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Max Planck Institute for Political and Social Science in Göttingen, Germany, is inviting applications for a Postdoctoral Fellow (m/f/d) in Political Science within its newly established Department of Political Institutions and Conflict. This is a full-time, three-year research position designed for highly qualified scholars who are interested in interdisciplinary, data-driven research on political institutions, conflict, and democratic transformation.

This postdoctoral opportunity is hosted under the broader umbrella of the Max Planck Society, one of the world’s leading research organizations, known for its excellence in scientific innovation, independent research, and global academic collaboration.

Overview of the Postdoctoral Fellowship

The Department of Political Institutions and Conflict focuses on understanding how political institutions shape conflict dynamics and democratic processes. The department explores critical global issues such as political violence, democratic erosion, and electoral integrity, with a strong empirical emphasis on the Global South.The position is designed for early-career researchers who are ready to contribute to cutting-edge academic research while developing their own independent scholarly trajectory within a collaborative and interdisciplinary environment.

Research Focus Areas

The department organizes its work around three major themes:

  1. Institutions and political violence
  2. Democratic politics in transformation
  3. Political economy of electoral integrity

Researchers are expected to engage with these themes through both quantitative and qualitative approaches. The department places particular emphasis on:

  • Survey-based research
  • Observational causal inference
  • Fieldwork and interviews
  • Analysis of administrative and event data

Key Responsibilities

Selected candidates will be expected to:

  • Conduct high-quality independent and collaborative research aligned with the department’s themes
  • Co-author academic articles with department members
  • Participate in fieldwork and conduct interviews in relevant research locations
  • Support the recruitment of PhD students and junior researchers
  • Co-supervise and mentor junior researchers and doctoral candidates
  • Assist in administrative and academic coordination tasks, including:
    • Organizing workshops
    • Managing data
    • Preparing project deliverables
    • Disseminating research findings to broader audiences
  • Actively participate in the department’s research environment
  • Maintain regular in-person presence in Göttingen, as residence within commuting distance is required

Candidates must have:

  • A PhD degree (or equivalent) in Political Science completed before the start date
  • A strong publication record in high-quality international journals or demonstrated potential for such output
  • Proven interest in the department’s research themes
  • Strong quantitative research skills, including:
    • Panel regression analysis
    • Handling large administrative datasets
    • Survey data analysis
    • Statistical programming in tools such as R or Stata
  • Excellent written and spoken English communication skills
  • Strong analytical thinking and independent research ability
  • A commitment to academic integrity and transparency
  • Ability to work both independently and collaboratively in interdisciplinary teams

Desirable Qualifications

The following will strengthen an application:

  • Familiarity with causal inference methodologies
  • Regional expertise in Global South contexts such as West Africa or South Asia
  • Additional language skills relevant to research regions (e.g., Hindi or Bengali)
  • Experience collecting, coding, and analyzing event data
  • Experience conducting fieldwork and qualitative interviews

Benefits of the Position

  • Full-time postdoctoral appointment for three years
  • Competitive salary aligned with the German public service wage agreement (TVöD Bund, EG 13), depending on experience
  • Access to a globally recognized research environment with advanced academic resources
  • Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration and international networking
  • No formal teaching obligations, allowing full focus on research
  • Possibility to engage with universities and teaching activities if desired

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit a single PDF file including:

  • Curriculum Vitae with a list of publications
  • A two-page cover letter outlining research interests and alignment with the department
  • A two-page research proposal related to the department’s themes
  • Contact details of three referees
  • One writing sample (published article or equivalent), with clarification of contribution if co-authored

Only complete applications submitted in a single PDF will be considered. No additional materials should be included.

Important Dates

  • Application Deadline: May 3, 2026
  • Interview Dates (remote): June 11–12, 2026
  • Expected Start Date: September 1, 2026 (or earlier if agreed)

Additional Information

  • Applicants of all nationalities are eligible to apply
  • Women are especially encouraged to apply as part of efforts to improve gender representation in science
  • Applicants with disabilities are strongly encouraged to apply under equal opportunity policies
  • The position requires residence within commuting distance of Göttingen
  • Regular physical presence at the institute is mandatory

This postdoctoral fellowship offers an exceptional opportunity to work at a premier research institution, contribute to impactful global research on political institutions and conflict, and develop a strong academic career in political science. Candidates with strong quantitative expertise, research experience, and interest in global political dynamics are especially encouraged to apply.

Application Deadline: May 3, 2026

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more fellowship opportunities , click here. 

The post Fully Funded Postdoctoral Fellowship in Political Science in Germany 2026 (Max Planck Institute) – Salary, Requirements, and How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
